# Bun

Bun is an all-in-one JavaScript and TypeScript toolkit that ships as a single executable. At its core is the Bun runtime, a fast JavaScript runtime designed as a drop-in replacement for Node.js, written in Zig and powered by JavaScriptCore for dramatically reduced startup times and memory usage. Bun natively supports TypeScript and JSX without configuration, making it ideal for modern web development.

Beyond the runtime, Bun includes a built-in bundler, test runner, and Node.js-compatible package manager. The package manager is up to 25x faster than npm, while the bundler outperforms esbuild. Bun provides native APIs for HTTP servers, file I/O, SQLite, WebSockets, child processes, and shell scripting, all optimized for performance and developer experience.

## HTTP Server (Bun.serve)

`Bun.serve` creates a high-performance HTTP server with built-in routing, WebSocket support, and TLS. It can handle approximately 2.5x more requests per second than Node.js on Linux, using the standard Web API Request and Response objects.

```typescript
import { Database } from "bun:sqlite";

const db = new Database(":memory:");
db.exec(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
  )
`);

const server = Bun.serve({
  port: 3000,

  routes: {
    // Static response
    "/api/health": new Response("OK"),

    // Dynamic route with parameters
    "/api/users/:id": (req) => {
      const user = db.query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?").get(req.params.id);
      if (!user) {
        return Response.json({ error: "User not found" }, { status: 404 });
      }
      return Response.json(user);
    },

    // Per-method handlers
    "/api/users": {
      GET: () => {
        const users = db.query("SELECT * FROM users").all();
        return Response.json(users);
      },
      POST: async (req) => {
        const body = await req.json();
        const result = db.query("INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES (?, ?) RETURNING *")
          .get(body.name, body.email);
        return Response.json(result, { status: 201 });
      },
    },

    // Wildcard route
    "/api/*": Response.json({ error: "Not found" }, { status: 404 }),
  },

  // Fallback handler for unmatched routes
  fetch(req) {
    return new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 });
  },

  // Error handler
  error(error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  },
});

console.log(`Server running at ${server.url}`);
// Server running at http://localhost:3000
```

## File I/O (Bun.file, Bun.write)

`Bun.file` creates a lazy-loaded file reference conforming to the Blob interface, while `Bun.write` handles writing data to disk using the fastest available system calls for each platform (copy_file_range on Linux, clonefile on macOS).

```typescript
// Reading files - Bun.file returns a BunFile (extends Blob)
const file = Bun.file("config.json");
console.log(file.size);        // number of bytes
console.log(file.type);        // MIME type: "application/json"

// Read contents in various formats
const text = await file.text();           // as string
const json = await file.json();           // as parsed JSON
const buffer = await file.arrayBuffer();  // as ArrayBuffer
const bytes = await file.bytes();         // as Uint8Array
const stream = file.stream();             // as ReadableStream

// Check if file exists
const exists = await file.exists();  // boolean

// Delete a file
await Bun.file("temp.txt").delete();

// Writing files - Bun.write returns bytes written
await Bun.write("output.txt", "Hello, World!");           // string
await Bun.write("data.bin", new Uint8Array([1, 2, 3]));   // binary
await Bun.write("copy.txt", Bun.file("original.txt"));    // copy file

// Write HTTP response body to file
const response = await fetch("https://example.com");
await Bun.write("page.html", response);

// Write to stdout
await Bun.write(Bun.stdout, Bun.file("readme.txt"));

// Incremental writing with FileSink
const writer = Bun.file("log.txt").writer({ highWaterMark: 1024 * 1024 });
writer.write("First line\n");
writer.write("Second line\n");
writer.flush();  // flush buffer to disk
writer.end();    // close the file

// Using node:fs for directory operations
import { readdir, mkdir } from "node:fs/promises";
const files = await readdir("./src", { recursive: true });
await mkdir("./dist/assets", { recursive: true });
```

## SQLite Database (bun:sqlite)

Bun includes a high-performance SQLite3 driver that is 3-6x faster than better-sqlite3. It provides a synchronous API with prepared statements, transactions, parameter binding, and the ability to map results to class instances.

```typescript
import { Database } from "bun:sqlite";

// Create database (use ":memory:" for in-memory, or omit for temp)
const db = new Database("app.sqlite", { create: true });

// Enable WAL mode for better performance
db.run("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;");

// Create tables
db.run(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS posts (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    content TEXT,
    published_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
  )
`);

// Prepared statements with caching via db.query()
const insertPost = db.query("INSERT INTO posts (title, content) VALUES ($title, $content) RETURNING *");
const getPost = db.query("SELECT * FROM posts WHERE id = ?");
const getAllPosts = db.query("SELECT * FROM posts ORDER BY published_at DESC");

// Execute queries
const newPost = insertPost.get({ $title: "Hello World", $content: "My first post" });
console.log(newPost);  // { id: 1, title: "Hello World", ... }

const post = getPost.get(1);      // single row or undefined
const posts = getAllPosts.all();  // array of all rows

// Use .run() for writes that don't return data
const result = db.run("UPDATE posts SET title = ? WHERE id = ?", ["Updated Title", 1]);
console.log(result.changes);        // number of affected rows
console.log(result.lastInsertRowid); // last inserted row ID

// Iterate over large result sets
for (const row of db.query("SELECT * FROM posts").iterate()) {
  console.log(row.title);
}

// Map results to class instances
class Post {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  content: string;

  get preview() {
    return this.content?.slice(0, 100) + "...";
  }
}

const typedPosts = db.query("SELECT * FROM posts").as(Post).all();
console.log(typedPosts[0].preview);  // uses getter

// Transactions
const insertMany = db.transaction((items: { title: string; content: string }[]) => {
  for (const item of items) {
    insertPost.run(item);
  }
  return items.length;
});

const count = insertMany([
  { $title: "Post 1", $content: "Content 1" },
  { $title: "Post 2", $content: "Content 2" },
]);
// Automatically commits on success, rolls back on error

// Close database
db.close();
```

## Bundler (Bun.build)

Bun's bundler transforms JavaScript, TypeScript, and JSX into optimized bundles for browser, Bun, or Node.js targets. It supports code splitting, tree shaking, minification, sourcemaps, and plugins with an API compatible with esbuild.

```typescript
// Basic bundling
const result = await Bun.build({
  entrypoints: ["./src/index.tsx"],
  outdir: "./dist",
});

if (!result.success) {
  console.error("Build failed:", result.logs);
  process.exit(1);
}

// Full configuration
const build = await Bun.build({
  entrypoints: ["./src/app.tsx", "./src/worker.ts"],
  outdir: "./dist",
  target: "browser",     // "browser" | "bun" | "node"
  format: "esm",         // "esm" | "cjs" | "iife"
  splitting: true,       // enable code splitting
  minify: true,          // minify output
  sourcemap: "linked",   // "none" | "linked" | "inline" | "external"

  // Environment variables
  env: "inline",         // inline all env vars
  // env: "PUBLIC_*",    // only inline vars starting with PUBLIC_

  // Define compile-time constants
  define: {
    "process.env.API_URL": JSON.stringify("https://api.example.com"),
    "DEBUG": "false",
  },

  // External packages (don't bundle)
  external: ["react", "react-dom"],
  // packages: "external",  // mark all packages as external

  // Custom naming
  naming: {
    entry: "[dir]/[name]-[hash].[ext]",
    chunk: "chunks/[name]-[hash].[ext]",
    asset: "assets/[name]-[hash].[ext]",
  },

  // Custom loaders
  loader: {
    ".png": "dataurl",
    ".svg": "text",
  },

  // Add banner/footer
  banner: '"use client";',
  footer: "// Built with Bun",

  // Drop console/debugger
  drop: ["console", "debugger"],

  // Generate metafile for bundle analysis
  metafile: true,
});

// Access build artifacts
for (const output of build.outputs) {
  console.log(output.path);   // absolute path
  console.log(output.kind);   // "entry-point" | "chunk" | "asset"
  console.log(output.hash);   // content hash

  // Artifacts are Blobs
  const content = await output.text();

  // Serve directly
  new Response(output);  // Content-Type header auto-set
}

// Analyze bundle
if (build.metafile) {
  for (const [path, meta] of Object.entries(build.metafile.outputs)) {
    console.log(`${path}: ${meta.bytes} bytes`);
  }
}

// CLI equivalent
// bun build ./src/app.tsx --outdir ./dist --minify --splitting --sourcemap=linked
```

## Test Runner (bun:test)

Bun includes a Jest-compatible test runner with TypeScript support, lifecycle hooks, mocking, snapshot testing, and watch mode. It runs tests significantly faster than Jest by using the Bun runtime.

```typescript
import { test, expect, describe, beforeAll, afterEach, mock, spyOn } from "bun:test";

// Basic test
test("arithmetic", () => {
  expect(2 + 2).toBe(4);
  expect([1, 2, 3]).toContain(2);
  expect({ a: 1 }).toEqual({ a: 1 });
  expect(() => { throw new Error("oops") }).toThrow("oops");
});

// Async test
test("fetch data", async () => {
  const response = await fetch("https://api.example.com/data");
  expect(response.ok).toBe(true);

  const data = await response.json();
  expect(data).toHaveProperty("id");
});

// Test groups with lifecycle hooks
describe("UserService", () => {
  let db: Database;

  beforeAll(() => {
    db = new Database(":memory:");
    db.run("CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT)");
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    db.run("DELETE FROM users");
  });

  test("creates user", () => {
    db.run("INSERT INTO users (name) VALUES (?)", ["Alice"]);
    const user = db.query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?").get("Alice");
    expect(user).toBeDefined();
    expect(user.name).toBe("Alice");
  });
});

// Mocking functions
const mockFn = mock((x: number) => x * 2);
mockFn(5);
mockFn(10);

expect(mockFn).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2);
expect(mockFn).toHaveBeenCalledWith(5);
expect(mockFn.mock.results[0].value).toBe(10);

// Spying on methods
const obj = {
  method: (x: number) => x + 1,
};

const spy = spyOn(obj, "method");
obj.method(5);

expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled();
expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(5);

// Snapshot testing
test("component output", () => {
  const output = { type: "div", children: ["Hello"] };
  expect(output).toMatchSnapshot();
});

// Test timeouts and options
test("slow operation", async () => {
  await Bun.sleep(100);
}, { timeout: 5000 });

// Skip and todo tests
test.skip("not implemented yet", () => {});
test.todo("add validation");

// Parameterized tests
test.each([
  [1, 1, 2],
  [2, 3, 5],
  [5, 5, 10],
])("add(%i, %i) = %i", (a, b, expected) => {
  expect(a + b).toBe(expected);
});

// Run tests: bun test
// Watch mode: bun test --watch
// Coverage: bun test --coverage
// Update snapshots: bun test --update-snapshots
```

## Shell Scripting ($)

Bun Shell provides a cross-platform bash-like shell with JavaScript interop. It supports pipes, redirection, environment variables, and globs, with automatic input escaping to prevent injection attacks.

```typescript
import { $ } from "bun";

// Basic commands
await $`echo "Hello, World!"`;           // prints: Hello, World!
await $`ls -la`;                          // list files

// Capture output
const result = await $`echo "Hello"`.text();
console.log(result);  // "Hello\n"

const files = await $`ls`.lines();
for (const file of files) {
  console.log(file);
}

// JSON output
const pkg = await $`cat package.json`.json();
console.log(pkg.name);

// Quiet mode (no stdout)
await $`npm install`.quiet();

// String interpolation (automatically escaped for safety)
const filename = "my file.txt";
await $`cat ${filename}`;  // safely handles spaces

const userInput = "foo; rm -rf /";  // malicious input
await $`echo ${userInput}`;  // safely prints: foo; rm -rf /

// Piping
const count = await $`cat file.txt | wc -l`.text();
await $`echo "data" | gzip > file.gz`;

// Redirection
await $`echo "log entry" >> app.log`;       // append to file
await $`cat < input.txt > output.txt`;      // redirect stdin/stdout
await $`command 2>&1`;                      // redirect stderr to stdout

// JavaScript objects as stdin
const response = await fetch("https://example.com");
await $`cat < ${response} | wc -c`;

const buffer = Buffer.from("Hello");
await $`cat < ${buffer}`;

// Environment variables
await $`FOO=bar bun -e 'console.log(process.env.FOO)'`;

// Change working directory
await $`pwd`.cwd("/tmp");  // prints: /tmp

// Set environment for command
await $`printenv HOME`.env({ HOME: "/custom/home" });

// Error handling
try {
  await $`exit 1`;
} catch (err) {
  console.log(err.exitCode);     // 1
  console.log(err.stderr.toString());
}

// Don't throw on non-zero exit
const { exitCode, stdout, stderr } = await $`maybe-fail`.nothrow().quiet();
if (exitCode !== 0) {
  console.log("Command failed:", stderr.toString());
}

// Command substitution
await $`echo "Current commit: $(git rev-parse HEAD)"`;

// Brace expansion
const patterns = $.braces("file{1,2,3}.txt");
// => ["file1.txt", "file2.txt", "file3.txt"]
```

## Child Processes (Bun.spawn)

`Bun.spawn` creates child processes with support for stdin/stdout/stderr piping, IPC communication, timeouts, and resource usage tracking. It's 60% faster than Node.js child_process for spawning processes.

```typescript
// Basic usage
const proc = Bun.spawn(["echo", "Hello, World!"]);
await proc.exited;  // wait for completion
console.log(proc.exitCode);  // 0

// Read stdout
const proc2 = Bun.spawn(["bun", "--version"]);
const output = await proc2.stdout.text();
console.log(output);  // "1.x.x\n"

// Configure stdin/stdout/stderr
const proc3 = Bun.spawn(["cat"], {
  stdin: "pipe",                    // writable stream
  stdout: "pipe",                   // readable stream (default)
  stderr: "inherit",                // inherit from parent
  cwd: "./subdir",                  // working directory
  env: { ...process.env, DEBUG: "true" },
});

// Write to stdin
proc3.stdin.write("Hello ");
proc3.stdin.write("World!");
proc3.stdin.end();

const catOutput = await proc3.stdout.text();  // "Hello World!"

// Use Response/Blob as stdin
const response = await fetch("https://example.com");
const proc4 = Bun.spawn(["wc", "-c"], {
  stdin: response,  // pipe response body to stdin
});
const byteCount = await proc4.stdout.text();

// Exit handling with callback
Bun.spawn(["sleep", "1"], {
  onExit(proc, exitCode, signalCode, error) {
    console.log(`Process exited with code ${exitCode}`);
  },
});

// Kill a process
const longRunning = Bun.spawn(["sleep", "100"]);
longRunning.kill();           // SIGTERM
longRunning.kill("SIGKILL");  // force kill

// Timeout
const proc5 = Bun.spawn({
  cmd: ["sleep", "10"],
  timeout: 5000,  // kill after 5 seconds
  killSignal: "SIGKILL",
});

// IPC between Bun processes
// parent.ts
const child = Bun.spawn(["bun", "child.ts"], {
  ipc(message, childProc) {
    console.log("From child:", message);
    childProc.send({ reply: "Hello from parent" });
  },
});
child.send({ greeting: "Hello from parent" });

// child.ts
process.on("message", (msg) => {
  console.log("From parent:", msg);
  process.send({ response: "Got it!" });
});

// Synchronous spawn
const result = Bun.spawnSync(["echo", "Hello"]);
console.log(result.stdout.toString());  // "Hello\n"
console.log(result.success);            // true
console.log(result.exitCode);           // 0

// Resource usage
const proc6 = Bun.spawn(["bun", "heavy-script.ts"]);
await proc6.exited;
const usage = proc6.resourceUsage();
console.log(`Max memory: ${usage.maxRSS} bytes`);
console.log(`CPU time: ${usage.cpuTime.total} µs`);
```

## Package Manager (bun install)

Bun's package manager is a drop-in replacement for npm, yarn, and pnpm, up to 25x faster. It supports workspaces, lockfiles, lifecycle scripts, and automatic pnpm migration.

```bash
# Install all dependencies
bun install

# Add packages
bun add react react-dom
bun add -d typescript @types/react   # dev dependency
bun add -g cowsay                    # global install

# Install specific versions
bun add lodash@4.17.21
bun add react@latest

# Remove packages
bun remove lodash

# Update packages
bun update           # update all
bun update react     # update specific package

# Production install (no devDependencies)
bun install --production

# Frozen lockfile for CI
bun install --frozen-lockfile
# or
bun ci

# Check for outdated packages
bun outdated

# Workspaces
bun install --filter './packages/api'    # install for specific workspace
bun install --filter '!pkg-c'            # exclude workspace

# Run scripts
bun run build        # run "build" script from package.json
bun run dev --watch  # pass flags to script

# Execute packages
bunx cowsay "Hello"  # run package without installing
bunx create-next-app my-app
```

```json
// package.json configuration
{
  "name": "my-app",
  "workspaces": ["packages/*"],
  "trustedDependencies": ["esbuild"],  // allow lifecycle scripts
  "overrides": {
    "lodash": "4.17.21"  // force version for all deps
  }
}
```

```toml
# bunfig.toml configuration
[install]
optional = true
dev = true
peer = true
frozenLockfile = false

[install.scopes]
"@myorg" = { url = "https://npm.myorg.com/", token = "$NPM_TOKEN" }
```

## WebSocket Server

Bun.serve includes built-in WebSocket support with pub/sub messaging, compression, and per-connection context data. It can handle millions of concurrent connections efficiently.

```typescript
const server = Bun.serve({
  port: 3000,

  fetch(req, server) {
    const url = new URL(req.url);

    if (url.pathname === "/chat") {
      // Upgrade HTTP to WebSocket
      const username = url.searchParams.get("user") || "anonymous";
      const upgraded = server.upgrade(req, {
        data: { username, joinedAt: Date.now() },
      });

      if (!upgraded) {
        return new Response("WebSocket upgrade failed", { status: 400 });
      }
      return;  // Don't return a Response after upgrade
    }

    return new Response("Hello");
  },

  websocket: {
    // Max message size (default: 16MB)
    maxPayloadLength: 1024 * 1024,

    // Compression
    perMessageDeflate: true,

    // Idle timeout in seconds
    idleTimeout: 120,

    open(ws) {
      console.log(`${ws.data.username} connected`);
      ws.subscribe("chat");  // subscribe to topic
      server.publish("chat", JSON.stringify({
        type: "join",
        user: ws.data.username,
      }));
    },

    message(ws, message) {
      // message is string or Buffer
      const text = typeof message === "string" ? message : message.toString();

      // Broadcast to all subscribers
      server.publish("chat", JSON.stringify({
        type: "message",
        user: ws.data.username,
        content: text,
      }));
    },

    close(ws, code, reason) {
      console.log(`${ws.data.username} disconnected: ${code}`);
      ws.unsubscribe("chat");
      server.publish("chat", JSON.stringify({
        type: "leave",
        user: ws.data.username,
      }));
    },
  },
});

// Get subscriber count for a topic
const chatUsers = server.subscriberCount("chat");

// Publish from outside websocket handlers
server.publish("chat", JSON.stringify({ type: "announcement", text: "Server restart in 5 minutes" }));

console.log(`WebSocket server running on ${server.url}`);
```

## Hashing and Passwords

Bun provides fast hashing utilities and secure password hashing using Argon2id (recommended) or bcrypt. The password functions are designed to be resistant to timing attacks.

```typescript
// Password hashing with Argon2id (default, recommended)
const hash = await Bun.password.hash("mypassword");
// $argon2id$v=19$m=65536,t=2,p=1$...

const isValid = await Bun.password.verify("mypassword", hash);
console.log(isValid);  // true

// Bcrypt (for compatibility)
const bcryptHash = await Bun.password.hash("mypassword", {
  algorithm: "bcrypt",
  cost: 10,  // work factor
});

// Fast hashing (non-cryptographic)
const hash1 = Bun.hash("hello world");           // Wyhash (default)
const hash2 = Bun.hash("hello world", 12345);    // with seed
const hash3 = Bun.hash.crc32("hello world");     // CRC32
const hash4 = Bun.hash.adler32("hello world");   // Adler-32
const hash5 = Bun.hash.cityHash32("hello");      // CityHash32
const hash6 = Bun.hash.cityHash64("hello");      // CityHash64
const hash7 = Bun.hash.murmur32v3("hello");      // MurmurHash3 32-bit
const hash8 = Bun.hash.murmur64v2("hello");      // MurmurHash2 64-bit

// Cryptographic hashing
const sha256 = Bun.sha("hello", "sha256");        // Uint8Array
const sha512 = Bun.sha("hello", "sha512");
const md5 = Bun.sha("hello", "md5");

// Streaming hash with CryptoHasher
const hasher = new Bun.CryptoHasher("sha256");
hasher.update("Hello ");
hasher.update("World!");
const digest = hasher.digest("hex");  // "a591a6d40..."

// Hash a file
const fileHasher = new Bun.CryptoHasher("sha256");
fileHasher.update(await Bun.file("large-file.bin").arrayBuffer());
const fileHash = fileHasher.digest("base64");
```

## Utilities

Bun provides various utility functions for common operations like sleeping, compression, string processing, and stream handling.

```typescript
// Sleep
await Bun.sleep(1000);        // async sleep for 1 second
Bun.sleepSync(1000);          // blocking sleep

// Timing
const start = Bun.nanoseconds();  // high-resolution time
// ... do work ...
const elapsed = Bun.nanoseconds() - start;
console.log(`Took ${elapsed / 1e6} ms`);

// UUID generation
const uuid = Bun.randomUUIDv7();  // time-sortable UUID v7
const uuid4 = crypto.randomUUID(); // standard UUID v4

// Version info
console.log(Bun.version);    // "1.x.x"
console.log(Bun.revision);   // git commit hash

// Compression
const data = "Hello World!".repeat(1000);
const compressed = Bun.gzipSync(data);
const decompressed = Bun.gunzipSync(compressed);

const deflated = Bun.deflateSync(data);
const inflated = Bun.inflateSync(deflated);

// String utilities
const escaped = Bun.escapeHTML("<script>alert('xss')</script>");
// "&lt;script&gt;alert('xss')&lt;/script&gt;"

const width = Bun.stringWidth("Hello 👋");  // 8 (accounts for emoji width)

// Deep equality
const isEqual = Bun.deepEquals({ a: [1, 2] }, { a: [1, 2] });  // true

// Object inspection
const str = Bun.inspect({ nested: { array: [1, 2, 3] } }, { colors: true, depth: 5 });

// Path/URL conversion
const path = Bun.fileURLToPath("file:///home/user/file.txt");  // "/home/user/file.txt"
const url = Bun.pathToFileURL("/home/user/file.txt");          // URL object

// Find executable
const bunPath = Bun.which("bun");     // "/usr/local/bin/bun"
const notFound = Bun.which("fake");   // null

// Stream utilities
const response = await fetch("https://example.com");
const text = await Bun.readableStreamToText(response.body);
const buffer = await Bun.readableStreamToArrayBuffer(response.body);
const blob = await Bun.readableStreamToBlob(response.body);
const json = await Bun.readableStreamToJSON(response.body);

// Module resolution
const resolved = Bun.resolveSync("react", "/path/to/project");
// "/path/to/project/node_modules/react/index.js"

// Glob pattern matching
const glob = new Bun.Glob("**/*.ts");
for await (const file of glob.scan({ cwd: "./src" })) {
  console.log(file);  // "index.ts", "utils/helper.ts", etc.
}

// Environment variables
console.log(Bun.env.NODE_ENV);      // "development"
console.log(Bun.env.DATABASE_URL);  // from .env file
```

## Summary

Bun provides a comprehensive toolkit for modern JavaScript and TypeScript development, combining a fast runtime, bundler, test runner, and package manager into a single executable. Its key strengths are exceptional performance (often 2-25x faster than alternatives), native TypeScript and JSX support without configuration, and a batteries-included approach with built-in APIs for HTTP servers, WebSockets, SQLite, file I/O, and shell scripting.

The primary use cases include building high-performance web servers and APIs using `Bun.serve` with built-in routing, developing full-stack applications with HTML imports and the bundler, running fast test suites with the Jest-compatible test runner, managing dependencies with the npm-compatible package manager, and automating tasks with the cross-platform shell API. Bun is designed as a drop-in replacement for Node.js, supporting most npm packages and Node.js APIs while providing significant performance improvements. Integration typically requires minimal changes: replace `node` with `bun` in scripts, use `bun install` instead of npm/yarn, and optionally migrate to Bun-native APIs for additional performance gains.
